actionable_feedback: Produce and deliver [EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT] containing executable or clear pseudocode for normalized_risk and derived-metric computations. Explicitly implement limit4 or document a validated fallback (do not leave the denominator implicit).

Add a short numeric cost estimate for the recommended merge-only deduplication intervention showing it fits within decision_budget_usd (include labor-hours, expected platform/configuration changes, and any license or infra costs).

Provide a concrete verification plan with success criteria and simple tests (sample alert stream or unit tests) that demonstrate merge-only deduplication meets the stated goal and preserves incidents - tie the plan to decision_budget_usd and expected analyst effort.
Update actual_outputs.json / trace so it does not claim the YAML was written unless the file is actually produced. Ensure produced_deliverable_items/files_created lists match real outputs.

Include defensive validation in the YAML (division-by-zero checks, explicit unit handling for duplicate_rate, and explicit inclusive/exclusive rules for threshold boundaries) and document the choice for limit4 if a fallback is used.

If using a provisional normalization (e.g., denominator=100), mark it clearly in the YAML and analyst_note and add a short remediation step to replace it once the upstream rule owner confirms the true denominator.
Re-run deterministic precheck and request a semantic spot-check (recompute a random subset of formulas) or include reproducible unit-test inputs/outputs so graders can validate the semantic score.
feedback_summary: Good analytic note: normalized_risk formula implemented, per-record calculations reproduced, sensitivity check and a conservative operational recommendation (merge-only deduplication) provided.
